philosophy.desc_short: Momentum comes from alternating activations, not waiting.
philosophy.desc_long: Rounds organize time, but momentum comes from who acts and who reacts. The battlefield evolves even if players hesitate.

initiative_logic.desc_short: Monsters activate first unless the scenario says otherwise.
initiative_logic.desc_long: Monster Factions always activate first. If both sides are Monsters, roll 1d6; high roll chooses. Initiative remains fixed for the game unless scenario-dictated.

activation_cycle.desc_short: Players alternate activating one model at a time.
activation_cycle.desc_long: Activations alternate between players, one model at a time, until all models have activated once.

the_activation.desc_short: Each model gets two actions per activation.
the_activation.desc_long: Each model receives two actions per activation. Actions may be repeated or mixed unless restricted.

round_definition.desc_short: A round ends once all models have activated.
round_definition.desc_long: A round is complete once all models on both sides have activated once.
